segments.text: As soon as he takes his birth in the world, a person who has so many responsibilities and obligations to the public, to the demigods, to the great sages, to the general living beings, to his parents, to his family forefathers, and to many others.
When he gives up all such obligations for the sake of the service of the Supreme Lord, he is not punished for such renunciation of obligation.
But if for sense gratification a person renounces all such obligations, he is punished by the law of God.
So here it is said, such a sadhu engages in staunch devotional service to the Lord without deviation.
Now, which sadhu is being referred to? The previous verse talks about what are the symptoms of a sadhu.
The symptoms of a sadhu are that he is tolerant, merciful, and friendly to all living entities.
He has no enemies, he is peaceful, he abides by the scriptures, and all his characteristics are sublime.
So what does this sadhu do with such symptoms?
For the sake of the service of the Lord, he renounces all other connections, such as family relationships and friendly acquaintances within the world.
Actually, to genuinely progress in spiritual life,
it is very important that we cultivate,
